title: Space Tech Briefing — July 08, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space explorers and future trailblazers! Welcome to your daily dose of the cosmos, broadcasting live on this incredible Wednesday, July 08, 2026. I'm your host, ready to launch you into the latest and greatest from across the universe!

We're starting our briefing with a look back at some truly stellar achievements that continue to shape our understanding of space.

First up, a huge shout-out to our friends at the Japanese Space Agency, JAXA! Their incredible Hayabusa-2 probe recently made another historic flyby. Just this past Sunday, July 5th, this phenomenal spacecraft zipped past the asteroid known as Torifune, officially designated (98943) 2001 CC21. JAXA even delighted us all by releasing the very first image of this close encounter on July 6th! What an incredible view! Hayabusa-2, launched way back in late 2014, has been an absolute triumph. You'll remember its groundbreaking rendezvous with asteroid Ryugu in 2018, where it successfully collected precious surface samples in 2019, bringing them safely back to Earth by late 2020. And thanks to some seriously impressive fuel savings, JAXA decided to extend this mission into what they're now calling Hayabusa2#! Simply brilliant, and it keeps on delivering!

And for those of you looking to add a touch of the cosmos to your everyday, let's not forget about the French National Centre for Space Studies, CNES! As we wait for today's new launches, let's look back at some recent developments. Their boutique is currently showcasing its summer essentials, perfect for stylish holidays with a distinct space-age flair. If you're looking to sport the colors of the French space agency, now's the time! There's a special summer offer running from July 1st all the way through to August 15th. So, if you're ready to infuse your summer with some cosmic style, you know where to look!

And that's a wrap on your morning space briefing! Thank you for joining us, keep your eyes on the skies, and have a truly stellar day!
